page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.large is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8369B cores running at 2.7 GHz, with 16.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 (specifically avx512f and avx512_vnni) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates average single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests. However, due to its low core count of 2 vCPUs, multi-core processing benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the lower performance tiers.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.r7.large offers a low-entry point for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power. It is suitable for small-scale databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring a high memory footprint relative to compute resources.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.large is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, delivering a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz, the instance utilizes the KVM hypervisor and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AES cryptography. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities but lower multi-core and memory bandwidth results due to the dual-core configuration. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads with modest compute requirements, such as small databases, caching systems, and lightweight enterprise applications.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, designed for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized dual-core virtual server featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for lightweight, memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Best suited for small databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy applications
